folio.label: 106r
folio.l: Wepyngö and waylyngö , care and other sorwe
I knowe I now , on Euen and on morwe
Quod the Marchaunt , and so don othere moo
That wedded ben , I trowe that it be so
Ful wel I wot , it faretú so by me
I haue a wyf , the werste that may be
For thougú the fend , to hire I coupled were
She wolde hi~ ouä macche , I dar wel swere
What shulde I ©ow reherce , in special
Hire heye malice , she is a shrewe witú al
There is a longö , and a large difference
Be twix Grisildis , grete pacience
And of my wyf , the passyngö cruelte
Were I vnbounden , also mot I the
I wolde neuäe eft , comen in the snare
We wedded men , lyue in sorwe and care
Assay who so wyl , and he shal fynde
That I seye sotú , be Seynt Thoms of Inde
As for the more ¹t , I seye nat alle
God shilde that it shulde , so byfalle
A goode sire hoost , I haue I wedded be
Theise Monthes two , and more nat parde
And ©et I trowe , that he that al his lyue
Wyflees hatú ben , thougú that men wold hi~ ryue
Vn to the hert , ne coude in no manere
Tellen so meche sorwe , as I now here
Coude tellen , of my wyues cursidnes
Now q¶ oure Host , Marchaunt so god ©ow blis
Syn ©e so mechil knowen , of that art
Ful hertily I pray ©ow , telle vs part
Gladly q¶ he , but of myn owen sore
For sory hert , I telle may nomore

note.notetxt: 32It seems that the first part of the prologue is written in a lighter brown than the rest. I suspect from line 21, but it is not a clear cut. The lighter brown is similar to the one on 105v, perhaps not the same of CO.ODR
